response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned Staff-level engineer with over 15 years of broad software experience, currently at Google contributing to MCP-based AI tooling and agentic workflows. However, the role mandates 8+ years of AI-driven solution development, and his AI-specific work is confined to his current Google role beginning April 2025 — roughly 13 months of AI exposure against an 8-year requirement. Python is entirely absent from his listed skills despite being the dominant language in applied GenAI. There is no demonstrated experience with RAG architectures, vector databases, LLM evaluation pipelines, or multi-agent frameworks such as LangGraph or CrewAI, all of which are explicitly required or strongly preferred qualifications for this position.
strengths: Staff-level engineering credibility at Google with hands-on involvement in Model Context Protocol tooling and agentic workflow infrastructure
Deep TypeScript/Node.js expertise with production-scale AWS cloud experience and high-throughput data pipeline work at Dunnhumby and Meta
Strong track record of mentoring, running workshops, and communicating technical concepts — directly relevant to the customer-facing leadership responsibilities of this role
concerns: Python is completely absent from the skills profile, a critical disqualifier for a GenAI engineering role where Python is the dominant ecosystem language
No evidence of RAG architecture, vector database usage, or LLM evaluation/optimization experience — all listed as required qualifications
AI engineering exposure is limited to approximately 13 months in the current role, falling significantly short of the 8-year minimum requirement for AI-driven solution development

key_factors.factor: Missing Python and RAG/vector DB experience
MCP and agentic tooling contribution at Google
